latest_week.brief_summary: A productive week with stdlib additions, bytecode fixes, parser improvements, and a long-pending physical comparison optimisation landing on trunk.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Dynarray gained iarray conversion functions, physical comparison operators now propagate to lambda, and record update syntax was relaxed to allow unparenthesized application expressions.
latest_week.new_features:
- [Dynarray.of_iarray and Dynarray.to_iarray](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14693) were added to the standard library, allowing round-tripping between dynamic and immutable arrays in O(n) time.
- [#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/13588) merged after a long review: physical comparison operators (`==` and `!=`) now propagate as a distinct `Pphyscomp` primitive to the lambda intermediate representation, paving the way for backends such as js_of_ocaml to emit semantically correct code for these operators.
- [#14692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14692) relaxed the record update syntax so that `{ (Gc.get ()) with ... }` no longer requires the surrounding parentheses around function application expressions.
- s390x CFI (Call Frame Information) debug metadata was improved via [#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14500), adding a new `cfi_val_offset` emission helper and GDB regression tests for the architecture.
- The `PUSHENVACC4` bytecode opcode, which was silently never emitted due to an off-by-one error in the peephole optimiser, is now correctly generated following [#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14534).
latest_week.activity_summary: Several PRs opened this week covering ocamltest infrastructure, typing error hints, thread-safety fixes in libunix, and ongoing discussions on continuation GC and Merlin typing recovery.
latest_week.activity:
- [#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14715) was opened by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) to fix NULL-dereference bugs in POSIX thread-safe functions in libunix, including `getgrnam_r` and related wrappers, with a cherry-pick request for OCaml 5.5.
- [#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14713) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposes a new `cold` action for ocamltest to handle tests that are disabled on CI by default but still run on developer machines.
- [#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14710) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) extends package type syntax to accept `with type t = int with type x = float` as a synonym for `with type t = int and type x = float`.
- [#14712](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14712) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) is an all-in-one variant of the expect test runner that executes normal, `-principal`, and `-rectypes` checks in a single process with state reset in between, showing no runtime regression.
- [#14707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14707) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) enables tracing of module-dependent functions in the REPL, so `#trace show;;` now works for functions with modular explicit arguments.
- [#14709](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14709) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) proposes changing ocamldoc HTML page titles from `Library name : Module_name` to `Module_name (Library name)`.
- [#14703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14703) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixes swapped `None`/`Some` branches in `dll.ml` when upgrading a DLL entry from Checking to Execution mode.
- [#14702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14702) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixed a bug in `Load_path.add` where files from a second hidden directory with overlapping basenames could leak into the visible files table; this was merged.
- [#14695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14695) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) adds an actionable hint to the GADT scope-escape error message, pointing users toward type annotations and the manual.
- [#14693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14693) adding `Dynarray.{of,to}_iarray` was merged.
- [#14692](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14692) relaxing record update syntax was merged.
- [#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14642) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) was merged, making the runtime silently skip `caml_shutdown` cleanup when unjoined domains remain rather than segfaulting; the superseded fatal-error approach [#14614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14614) was closed.
- [#14641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14641) moved `opnames.ml.c` and `opnames.mli` from `tools/` into `bytecomp/` to resolve a dependency ordering issue.
- [#14690](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14690) by [cui](https://github.com/cuiweixie) fixed an ambiguous type path expansion bug in error trace reporting.
- The long-running [#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14241) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) (Merlin typing recovery upstreaming) saw an active review round with [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) approving the overall structure.
latest_week.discussions_summary: Discussions this week centred on continuation garbage collection semantics, the ppxlib request for new AST extension points, and CI workflow security hardening.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14580) on GC for continuations saw a new proposal: rather than `discontinue`-ing unreachable continuations from finalisers (which raises ordering concerns), a first step could simply free the fiber stack memory and optionally provide an explicit `Gc.flush()` API for users who need finalisation at a known point.
- [Issue #14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) on adding extension point entries to the OCaml AST to aid ppxlib's frozen-AST strategy received a constructive response from [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), suggesting a concrete PR ahead of an upcoming developer meeting.
- [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14676) on preventing shell injection in GitHub Actions workflows is stalled over a subtle issue where environment variable names conflict with GitHub's own reserved names (e.g., `GITHUB_REF_NAME`), complicating the security fix.
- [Issue #14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) on parallel test segfaults in cleanup-at-exit mode continued: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) tracked down a race condition in `ephetest_par` where domains can deadlock waiting on ephemerons, and reproduced it reliably by enabling GC verbose logging.
- [Issue #14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655) on overflow in `Bigarray.reshape` now has a CVE assigned: CVE-2026-34353.
latest_week.trends_summary: Continued attention to correctness and observability in the multicore runtime, alongside incremental improvements to the bytecode compiler pipeline.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- The runtime team is working through several multicore-specific correctness issues: continuation memory leaks ([#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14580)), cleanup-at-exit races ([#14642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14642)), and ephemeron GC liveness ([#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594)).
- Multiple bytecode-related fixes landed this week ([#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14534), [#14641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14641), [#13588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/13588)), reflecting increased attention to the bytecode backend's correctness and its use as a target by tools like js_of_ocaml.
- The ppxlib ecosystem is pushing for upstream changes to ease long-term AST stability, with [issue #14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) opening a formal discussion track.
latest_week.issues_summary: A CVE was assigned for a Bigarray overflow, and new flaky CI failures were reported on Windows and ARM64.
latest_week.good_first_issues:
- [Issue #14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) requests support for unit suffixes in `OCAMLRUNPARAM` memory parameters (e.g., `s=2MB` or `s=2Mw`) to reduce user confusion; labelled `good first issue`.
- [Issue #14708](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14708) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reports an intermittent Dynlink test failure on the MSVC 64-bit CI, labelled `flaky-ci-failure` and `windows`.
- [Issue #14687](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14687) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) tracks a still-flaky `test_dropped_events` runtime events test on linux-arm64, cross-referenced with an oxcaml PR.

latest_week.brief_summary: Three PRs under active review: Nushell shell support, action list UI reordering, and symlink handling in file:// repos.
latest_week.activity_summary: Three open PRs received review feedback covering shell portability, UI improvements, and repository backend fixes.
latest_week.activity:
- **Nushell shell support** PR [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873) by [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) continues review; addresses `opam env` integration via JSON output (`load-env (opam env | to json)`) and shell config setup, continuing work from [#6330](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6330); [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) requested a minimum version check, a user-visible warning about nushell's rapid evolution, and updated reftest coverage in `tests/reftests/init-scripts.unix.test`
- **Action list reordering** PR [#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) proposes sorting solver actions by priority (fixes [#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863)), with [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) requesting downgrades be shown after upgrades and a comment explaining the ordering rationale
- **Symlink handling in `file://` repositories** PR [#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6874) by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) replaces `Unix.lstat` with `Unix.stat` in `get_diff` so symlinks are followed transparently, fixing `Symlinks are unsupported` errors seen in opam-repository CI on Windows/Cygwin (related to [#6410](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6410)); [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) noted that symlink support was intentionally removed in 2.4 and flagged that the same code path is being rewritten in [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)
- **DockerHub image documentation** issue [#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875) opened by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) questioning why images advertise the latest opam but ship `opam 2.0.10` by default; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) clarified that `/usr/bin/opam-<version>` holds newer releases and the default symlink targets the lowest supported version for broad test coverage, then updated the DockerHub description accordingly and closed the issue
latest_week.discussions_summary: DockerHub image versioning prompted a clarification on opam's intentional multi-version testing strategy.
latest_week.notable_discussions: - **DockerHub opam versioning** ([#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875)): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) raised confusion over images shipping `opam 2.0.10` despite being described as current; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explained the design—`/usr/bin/opam` points to the lowest supported release to maximise test coverage, while `/usr/bin/opam-<version>` (e.g. `/usr/bin/opam-2.5`) provides the latest—and updated the DockerHub page text to make this explicit; the broader policy shift to make 2.1.0 the minimum tested version is tracked at the [OCaml Discuss thread](https://discuss.ocaml.org/t/proposal-make-the-minimum-tested-opam-2-1-and-higher/17736)
latest_week.trends_summary: Review activity focuses on shell portability, solver UI clarity, and repository backend robustness.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Shell portability expansion**: the Nushell PR [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873) continues a pattern of extending opam's shell integration beyond traditional POSIX shells, building on prior work in [#6330](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6330)
- **Repository backend hardening**: the symlink fix PR [#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6874) and the ongoing rewrite in [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) reflect sustained effort to make the `file://` and local repository backends more robust across platforms
- **Solver output clarity**: [#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864) and its parent [#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863) signal attention to making the action plan presented to users easier to interpret at a glance
latest_week.contributors_summary: Five contributors active through PR authorship, review comments, and issue triage.
latest_week.contributors:
- [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) iterated on the Nushell support PR [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873), addressing reviewer feedback on version checks, quoting in `export_in_shell`, macOS/Windows config path handling, and shell hook setup
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) authored the action list reordering PR [#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864) fixing [#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863)
- [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) authored the symlink fix PR [#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6874) with detailed analysis of edge cases (dangling symlinks, circular symlinks) and new cram test coverage
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) provided review feedback on both [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873) and [#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6874), including design context from the 2.4 release and pointers to the ongoing rewrite in [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625), and requested ordering commentary on [#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864)
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened and participated in the DockerHub versioning discussion [#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875)
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) responded to [#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875), clarified the multi-version testing rationale, updated the DockerHub page description, and closed the issue

latest_week.brief_summary: Hover improvements for class/object constructs merged, signature-help readability change reverted, and Dune 3.22 build fix applied.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Hover now works on class declarations, class fields, object expressions, and record expression brackets.
latest_week.new_features:
- **Hover support for class and object constructs** expanded by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos): hovering over class declaration names, class type declarations, class descriptions, class fields (`val`/`method`), inherited class names, `class_expr` constructors, and `class_type` constructors now triggers type-enclosing queries. Also added hover on `Pexp_new` expressions and the opening/closing brackets of record expressions. Addresses [#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587) and merged as [#1599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1599).
- **Missing `Texp_new` hover case** added in a follow-up commit ([990063d](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/commit/990063d9560ded20315303859570c40897fdda2c)) by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), covering object instantiation expressions that were not previously handled by the hover filter.

latest_week.discussions_summary: Discussion on PR #1579 clarified that -real-paths is Merlin-specific and the default short-paths behavior should match the rest of the stack.

latest_week.brief_summary: Improved type enclosing for classes/objects merged, plus ongoing indexer performance work with LRU cache and pointing indexes.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Type enclosing now covers class types and objects; type annotation fix prepares for upcoming OCaml keep-expansion feature.
latest_week.new_features:
- **Type enclosing improvements for classes and objects**: a new `Classtype` variant added to the `type_info` type in `type_enclosing.mli`, with expanded logic in `type_enclosing.ml` and `browse_raw.ml` to handle class types, class type aliases, and object expressions. This addresses [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587) and substantially improves the editor experience when working with OCaml class-based code ([#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053))
- **Forward-compatibility fix for `Destruct.refine_complete_match`**: a type annotation was added to the `patterns` parameter to avoid a type scope escape that will become a hard error with the upcoming `keep-expansion` feature in `ocaml/ocaml#11648`. The fix ensures Merlin continues to build correctly across OCaml 5.4, 5.5, and 5.6 ([#2054](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2054))
latest_week.activity_summary: New PRs opened for 5.7.0-504 changelog and indexer memory optimizations; older PPX-expand and jump-list PRs were closed.
latest_week.activity:
- **Changelog preparation for 5.7.0-504** opened by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) to document the upcoming release ([#2055](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2055))
- **Pointing indexes for `ocaml-index`** under active review: [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d)'s PR adds support for index files that reference another on-disk index rather than duplicating data, yielding ~2.7x write performance improvement in benchmarks with similar read performance; [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) is providing detailed review feedback on race conditions and ID-checking logic ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **LRU cache for indexer memory usage** under review: [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)'s PR replaces an unbounded `Hashtable` in the indexer with an LRU cache to bound memory consumption; benchmarks show ~1.1x memory increase without the eviction step, discussion ongoing about cache correctness ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050))
- **Dependabot `cachix/install-nix-action` bump** from 31.8.4 to 31.10.3 opened and awaiting merge ([#2052](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2052)); an earlier bump to 31.10.1 ([#2048](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2048)) was closed in favour of this newer version
- **`get_all` jump targets PR closed**: [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust)'s PR implementing a single-trip query returning all jump targets was closed without merging due to conflicts ([#1891](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1891))
latest_week.discussions_summary: Active review discussions on race conditions in pointing indexes and correctness of LRU cache eviction.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) identified a potential race condition in the pointing-index implementation ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): using `with_open` to check a file identifier before opening it again to read provides no safety guarantee if the file is replaced between the two opens. The recommendation is to check an already-open file descriptor so the same contents are guaranteed during reading.
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) flagged a cache correctness issue in the LRU PR ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): removing a cache entry when an offset is no longer needed loses sharing, as the same value won't be reintroduced later, causing duplicate allocations. Benchmarks show a ~1.1x memory penalty without the removal.
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) noted that the type-enclosing class/object improvement ([#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053)) warrants a changelog entry, which was subsequently added before merging.
latest_week.trends_summary: Sustained focus on ocaml-index scalability, with two concurrent PRs addressing memory and write-performance from different angles.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- Two independent PRs ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050), [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) are simultaneously targeting `ocaml-index` performance from complementary directions: bounded memory via LRU eviction and reduced write volume via pointer-based index sharing. This suggests a coordinated push to make the indexer viable for larger codebases.
- Proactive forward-compatibility work for upcoming OCaml compiler features continues: [#2054](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2054) addresses a type-scope escape that will become an error in a future compiler version, following the pattern of keeping Merlin ahead of compiler changes.
latest_week.contributors_summary: Four contributors active this week across code, reviews, and discussions.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) authored and merged the type enclosing improvements for classes and objects ([#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053)), adding the `Classtype` variant and expanding browse logic across multiple files
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) contributed the forward-compatibility type annotation fix in `Destruct.refine_complete_match` ([#2054](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2054)) and worked through CI feedback on cross-version compatibility
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) provided in-depth review on both the pointing-index PR ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) and the LRU cache PR ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)), raising correctness concerns around race conditions, eviction semantics, and random identifier quality
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) reviewed [#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053) and prompted the addition of a changelog entry before merging
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) opened the changelog PR for 5.7.0-504 ([#2055](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2055)) and had an older jump-targets PR ([#1891](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1891)) closed this week
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) continued iterating on the pointing-index implementation ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)), addressing reviewer feedback with benchmark data and code fixes
- [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) continued work on the LRU-based indexer PR ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)), providing benchmark data in response to review questions

latest_week.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.1 was released, with new features including DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT env var, relocatable compiler support, Windows hardlink sandboxing, and c_library_flags for foreign_library.
latest_week.new_features_summary: New features include the DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable for actions, relocatable compiler detection for package management, Windows hardlink sandboxing, and c_library_flags support in foreign_library stanzas.
latest_week.new_features:
- **DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable** ([#13934](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13934)): Actions can now observe their project root via the `DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT` env var, avoiding the need to pass `%{project_root}` manually and preventing the path from being recorded in the action digest — merged by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Relocatable compiler support in package management** ([#13321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13321)): Dune can now detect if a compiler in its package universe is relocatable (via the `relocatable-compiler` meta-package or OCaml 5.5+), enabling the compiler to be built and cached like a regular package instead of using toolchains — merged by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Hardlink sandboxing on Windows** ([#13987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13987)): The restriction on using hardlink sandboxing on Windows has been lifted, with symlink sandboxing attempts now failing with a clearer error message — merged by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), partially addressing [#4362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4362)
- **`c_library_flags` in `foreign_library`** ([#13484](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13484)): The `c_library_flags` field is now supported in `foreign_library` stanzas, allowing flags to be passed to `ocamlmklib` — merged by [@madroach](https://github.com/madroach)
- **Improved `diff` action inference** ([#14008](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14008)): Source directories are now automatically inferred as dependencies when referenced as the left-hand side in `diff` actions — merged by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Multiple `(dirs ..)` stanzas** ([#13734](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13734)): Support for multiple `(dirs ..)` stanzas in the same dune file, with intersection semantics starting from dune language version 3.23 — merged by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), fixing [#6249](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/6249)
latest_week.activity_summary: Active week combining the 3.22.1 patch release with substantial ongoing work on the digest/marshal refactor, sandbox fixes, and relocatable compiler integration.
latest_week.activity:
- **Open PRs under active review**: [#14051](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14051) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposes replacing `marshal`-based digests with `repr`-derived functions for improved robustness and boot speed; [#13337](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13337) by [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht) adds a `scope` stanza for library visibility control when vendoring; [#14021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14021) and [#14030](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14030) by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) work toward finer per-module dependency analysis between libraries ([#4572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4572))
- **Dune 3.22.1 released** ([#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13947)): Patch release merged by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), fixing Windows 7 compatibility, a crash in `dune test` without a default context ([#13930](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13930), [#13904](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13904)), and Chrome trace format timestamps ([#13911](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13911))
- **Sandbox fix: honor sandbox config from `(:include ..)` deps** ([#13898](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13898)): Sandbox specifications inside `(:include foo.sexp)` dependency expressions were previously ignored — fixed by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Melange fix: stage `.cmj` files in sandbox with `--mel-cross-module-opt`** ([#14018](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14018)): Fix by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **RPC error message improvement** ([#13983](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13983)): Clarified the error shown when another dune instance is running to read "Another Dune instance is currently running. Aborting..." — contributed by [Meet Zalavadiya](https://github.com/meet0806), fixing [#13902](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13902)
- **Scheduler: ignore unset SIGCHLD signals on macOS** ([#14047](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14047)): Fixed a crash during SIGINT cleanup on macOS where `SIGCHLD` handling received garbage signals — merged by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **Digest caching fix: use device/inode** ([#13991](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13991)) and inode storage fix ([#14058](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14058)): Digest cache now invalidates based on device and inode numbers rather than floating-point mtime approximations, and the inode field was corrected (previously stored the device instead) — by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Diff action fix with directories in `_build`** ([#13970](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13970)): Fixed [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **CI: line-ending check added** ([#14013](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14013)): Detects files committed with incorrect line endings (a recurring issue for Windows/`jj` users) — merged by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan), closing [#14006](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14006)
- **Rocq updated to 9.2** ([#13996](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13996)): Drops OCaml 4 from Rocq support — merged by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Revdep workflow improvements**: Made reusable ([#14014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14014)) and fixed multi-target invocation ([#14024](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14024)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV); fixed run on untagged versions ([#14000](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14000))
- **Backporting script added** ([#13952](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13952)): Automates most of the work of cherry-picking fixes to release candidate branches — merged by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- **Extensive test digest cleanup**: Multiple PRs ([#14032](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14032), [#14033](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14033), [#14034](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14034), [#14035](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14035), [#14036](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14036), [#14041](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14041), [#14043](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14043), [#14048](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14048), [#14050](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14050)) remove concrete digest values from tests to reduce test churn — by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
latest_week.discussions_summary: Discussions this week centered on RPC/lock behavior for concurrent dune invocations, the approach to per-module library dependency analysis, and the scope stanza for vendoring control.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- **Concurrent dune invocations and RPC locking** ([#13964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13964)): Debate between [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) on whether dune should retry on lock contention vs. fail immediately; the thread notes AI agent workflows as a key motivating use case, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) arguing immediate failure is actually better for agent loops
- **Finer library dependency analysis** ([#14021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14021)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) is implementing per-module dependency filtering using `ocamldep` output to avoid full recompilation of a library when only some of its dependencies change; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) are actively reviewing the prerequisite PRs ([#14017](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14017), [#14030](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14030), [#14031](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14031))
- **`scope` stanza for library visibility** ([#13337](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13337)): [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht) is proposing a `(scope (dir ..) (packages ..))` stanza to control library visibility when vendoring; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) raised concerns about long-term maintenance commitment before accepting new dune language features
- **`(generate_project_file)` incompatibility with VSRocq** ([#13738](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13738)): [Marcin Wojnarowski](https://github.com/shilangyu) reported that the generated `_RocqProject` file does not work correctly with VSRocq's import resolution under `(include_subdirs qualified)`, and offered to contribute a fix pending guidance from Rocq plugin maintainers
- **macOS SIGCHLD signal handling** ([#14023](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14023)): [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) investigated unreliable signal delivery on macOS during high-concurrency builds; the mitigation of ignoring unset SIGCHLD signals was merged ([#14047](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14047)) while the broader fix using a C-stub self-pipe remains in draft
- **Deprecating `(mode promote)`** ([#14044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14044)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed deprecating the `promote` rule mode, arguing both its use cases are better addressed by `diff` actions and a planned `--promote-file` build flag
- **opam-ci support roadmap** ([#14020](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14020)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened an issue to plan coordination with opam infrastructure as dune package management becomes more widely recommended
latest_week.trends_summary: The week shows a sustained push to replace marshal-based digest computation with type-safe repr-derived functions, reduce test fragility, and advance Windows parity.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Marshal-to-repr digest migration**: A multi-PR effort across the week ([#14015](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14015), [#14016](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14016), [#14025](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14025), [#14034](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14034)) is replacing generic `marshal`-based digest functions with `repr`-derived type-specific ones; the culminating open PR [#14051](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14051) claims improved boot speed as a side effect
- **Windows parity improvements**: Hardlink sandboxing now enabled ([#13987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13987)), line-ending CI check added ([#14013](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14013)), and the Windows path-rewriting crash ([#10176](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10176)) has a reproduction test ([#13988](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13988)); [#11161](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11161) continues to track the full Windows package management story
- **Relocatable compiler integration**: Following the merge of [#13321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13321), issue [#14012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14012) outlines the next step of preferring the relocatable compiler by default, reducing the need for toolchain rebuilds
- **Release process automation**: The backport script ([#13952](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13952)), reusable revdep workflow ([#14014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14014)), and OxCaml CI build PR ([#13981](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13981)) indicate ongoing investment in reducing manual release overhead

latest_week.brief_summary: Discussion restarted on the implicit source positions RFC ([#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)), with a push to resolve the stalled design debate.
latest_week.activity_summary: Active discussion on the long-stalled implicit source positions RFC, with a proposal to bring it to the caml-devel meeting.
latest_week.activity: - The **implicit source positions** RFC [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) saw renewed discussion after a year of silence, with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) summarizing the outstanding design question: whether to use `Lexing.position` (option 1) or a richer location type (option 2b), and expressing a preference for 2b with option 1 added only if performance concerns are demonstrated. [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) suggested bringing the discussion to the next caml-devel meeting to break the deadlock.
latest_week.discussions_summary: Debate on source position representation in RFC #52 centers on Lexing.position vs. richer location types, with a caml-devel meeting proposed to resolve it.
latest_week.notable_discussions: - The core unresolved question in [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) is the **representation of call-site positions**: option 1 (a simple `Lexing.position`) vs. option 2b (a richer, smarter location type). [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) argues for 2b as the default, noting that performance concerns for option 1 remain unsubstantiated, while [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) recommends escalating to the caml-devel meeting to reach a concrete decision.
latest_week.trends_summary: Long-stalled RFCs with prior consensus are being actively revived to reach final decisions.
latest_week.emerging_trends: - The **implicit source positions** RFC [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) illustrates a pattern of features that have accumulated design consensus but stalled on implementation details; contributors are now pushing to resolve these via structured meetings rather than async PR discussion.
latest_week.contributors_summary: Olivier Nicole and Nicolás Ojeda Bär participated in discussion on RFC #52.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) re-engaged the discussion on [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) after a year of inactivity, summarizing the design tradeoffs and proposing a path forward with option 2b.
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) responded to the discussion on [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52), acknowledging the need to get unstuck and proposing the caml-devel meeting as the venue to reach a decision.

latest_week.brief_summary: Two new PRs opened: OxCaml unboxed type support and @inline tag for module declarations.
latest_week.activity_summary: Two unmerged PRs advancing OxCaml compatibility and inline module rendering, with renewed discussion on issue #564.
latest_week.activity:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened [#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407) **OxCaml: Support for unboxed named types**, a follow-up to [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404) that fixes missing trailing `#` in generated documentation for OxCaml unboxed types by propagating the `Pextra_ty (_, Punboxed_ty)` representation through odoc internals (757 additions across 84 files)
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) **Add `@inline` tag support for module declarations**, implementing the feature requested in [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564) since 2021 — modules annotated with `@inline` in their doc comment have their contents rendered directly on the parent page, reusing the existing inline include rendering path; the PR adds `inline : bool` to `Lang.Module.t`, threads it through `Component.Module.t` and xref2, and includes test cases for HTML, LaTeX, man, and markdown generators
- Issue [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564) **Support inlining expansions in their parent's page** received renewed activity with [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) commenting to reference [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) as a proof-of-concept implementation
latest_week.discussions_summary: Issue #564 on inline module rendering saw renewed engagement after five years, prompted by PR #1408.
latest_week.notable_discussions: - The long-standing issue [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564) on **inlining submodule expansions** into the parent page was revisited by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) with a concrete implementation in [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408), reviving discussion about whether inline-by-default vs. opt-in behaviour is preferable for ecosystem consistency
latest_week.trends_summary: Ongoing OxCaml compatibility work and renewed focus on inline rendering of small submodules.
latest_week.emerging_trends:

- **Inline module rendering** is gaining renewed attention through [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) and [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564), exploring how to give users control over whether small submodules are rendered inline or on a separate page
latest_week.contributors_summary: Martin Jambon opened PR #1408 and commented on issue #564; @art-w opened PR #1407.

latest_week.discussions_summary: A missing exenum package was flagged, and curses 1.0.12 required multiple review rounds to fix OCaml/dune lower bounds and Windows incompatibility.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [#29646](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29646) Issue raised by [Dmitrii Kosarev (@Kakadu)](https://github.com/Kakadu) reporting that `exenum.0.84` and its upstream GitHub repository are missing (404), with comments also noting missing `azure-cosmos-db.0.1.3` and `omtl`
- [#29634](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29634) curses 1.0.12 received detailed review from [Jan Midtgaard (@jmid)](https://github.com/jmid) identifying a `Val_none` compile error on OCaml < 4.12, a dune lower bound requiring >= 3.17, and a `LC_MESSAGES` symbol missing on Windows — leading to the package being restricted to non-Windows platforms
- [#29644](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29644) A constraint PR from [Vincent Balat (@balat)](https://github.com/balat) to restrict `ocsigen-i18n < 5.0.0` for `ocsigen-start 8.0.0` was closed after the new ocsigen-i18n 5.0.0 PR ([#29643](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29643)) was opened instead
latest_week.trends_summary: Windows/MSYS2 packaging improvements continued, and formal methods tooling (binsec, bitwuzla, smtml, aslref) saw active development.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Formal methods and SMT solvers** had an active week with binsec 0.11.1, bitwuzla 1.0.6 (with quantifiers), smtml 0.24.0, and the new ARM ASL reference implementation aslref 1.0-EAC1 all landing
- **Windows compatibility work** was prominent with conf-brotli Windows fix ([#29645](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29645)), MSYS2 depext improvements ([#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29579)), and Perl conf packages Cygwin/MSYS2 support ([#29613](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29613)) in progress
- **Observability** saw opentelemetry 0.90 land with a major internal refactor splitting signal providers — a sign of maturing OCaml telemetry infrastructure
- **Cryptography** saw sm 0.1.0, a pure OCaml implementation of Chinese national cryptographic algorithms (SM2/SM3/SM4/ZUC), as a novel addition to the ecosystem

latest_week.brief_summary: Three compiler and Wasm bug fixes merged by Jérôme Vouillon, addressing reference unboxing and Wasm direct call issues.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Bug fixes for reference unboxing, Wasm empty closure identity, and Wasm stale global data flow in direct calls.
latest_week.new_features:
- Fixed a **substitution bug in reference unboxing** that caused an assertion failure in `compiler/lib/js_assign.ml` when compiling with containers-data 3.18, reported by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) in [#2209](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2209) and resolved by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [#2210](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2210)
- Fixed **Wasm physical identity of empty closures** so that function equality semantics are correctly preserved in the Wasm backend ([#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207))
- Added a **guard for stale global data flow data** in the Wasm backend to prevent crashes on certain direct function calls ([#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208))
latest_week.activity_summary: Three bug fix PRs merged; open work includes Dynlink/toplevel Wasm support, compiler speedup, OCaml 5.5 support, and ESM tooling.
latest_week.activity:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) continues work on **Dynlink and toplevel support** for the Wasm backend, a large in-progress PR with over 6000 lines of changes ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) has an open PR for **compiler substitution speedup** aimed at reducing compile times ([#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185)), and a draft PR for **initial OCaml 5.5 runtime and compiler support** with extensive changes across 73 files ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) also has a draft PR for **initial ECMAScript modules (ESM) tooling** in the JS compiler backend ([#2163](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2163))

latest_week.brief_summary: A highly active week with major advances in staging/GADTs, abstract kinds, new modes, Flambda2 optimizations, and backend codegen improvements.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Key new language features include abstract kind constraints, new write/corrupted/corruptible modes, static modules, and sound GADTs with staging.
latest_week.new_features:
- **Abstract kinds** gained `with kind_` constraints via [#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629), completing the core feature set for abstract kinds before a formal announcement.
- **New modes** `write` and `writing` were added in [#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608), and `corrupted` and `corruptible` modes (the contention/portability analogues) followed in [#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713), enabling cleaner mode implications without needing `shared`.
- **Static modules** landed via [#5724](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5724) and the underlying static computation framework via [#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430).
- **Staging and GADTs** became mostly sound together through the combined parts 1–3 PR [#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641), with stage tracking in `Ctype` across quotes/splices and exhaustive GADT tests.
- **Quotes of expansive expressions** are now typed at mode `once` in [#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), and top-level `Pstr_eval` expressions can be at mode `once` via [#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715).
- **Sound moding of quotes and splices** with tests was merged via [#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697).
- **Type checking `let poly_`** landed in [#5513](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5513), with a companion PR adding `val poly_` support opened as [#5755](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5755).
- **jkind annotation errors** in quotes are now properly raised rather than silently dropped, with assertions added in `Translquote` via [#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674).
- **X86 peephole optimizations** infrastructure was merged in [#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639), adding new rules for dead-register removal and redundant comparison elimination.
- **New `Lfor` compilation strategy** was introduced in [#5728](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5728).
- A **complexity threshold for IRC** register allocation was added in [#5705](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5705), allowing fallback to a simpler allocator for large functions.
- A new **`-thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization`** flag was added for wasm_of_ocaml support in [#5720](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5720).
- **`Atomic.make_contended`** was made to work on runtime4 in [#5640](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5640).
- **GC stat samples** now record current minor words in [#5030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5030).
- **`Bigarray` construction routines** are proposed to return `@ unique` in [#5767](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5767), and read operations are proposed to require only `@ read` access in [#5768](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5768).
- **Bug fix**: allocation size for recursive mixed blocks on bytecode was corrected in [#5754](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5754), preventing segfaults with void fields.
- **Bug fix**: tricky parallel Memprof bugs were fixed in [#5600](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5600).
- **Bug fix**: `moregeneral` now correctly regeneralizes layout vars on error via [#5691](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5691).
- **Bug fix**: the n-way join correctly handles joins of symbol projections in [#5745](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5745).
- **Bug fix**: `value_or_null` indices can now be deepened in [#5735](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5735).
- **Performance**: `caml_modify` calls are reduced when writing parameterized unboxed records in [#5736](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5736).
- **Performance**: the n-way join now uses untagged const false to detect nullability in [#5718](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5718).
latest_week.activity_summary: A very busy week with 47 merged PRs spanning staging, modes, Flambda2, reaper, backend codegen, and the ongoing OxCaml 5.4 merge track.
latest_week.activity:
- [#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774) proposes representing all-value/void **mixed records and variants as uniform blocks**, bumping mixed block version to v6 and removing the 254-value limit.
- [#5734](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5734) adds initial (untested) **aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu support in the dissector**; a related issue [#5732](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5732) was opened to track dissector support for arm64 Linux.
- [#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738) proposes **improved codegen for int comparisons** using XOR before comparison and a better 3-way comparison lowering.
- [#5746](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5746) proposes **eliminating useless shifts in string/bytes indexed access**.
- [#5740](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5740) proposes **duplicating comparisons in CFG block terminators** for select/cmov.
- [#5742](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5742) opens work on **unwinding support via sframes**.
- [#5751](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5751) continues work on the n-way join adding **extra params for CSE-like variables at join points**, a reloaded version of [#5733](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5733).
- [#5771](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5771) fixes a **bug in meet for array element kinds** in Flambda2.
- [#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770) proposes using **callback modules to emulate function specialisation** in the patricia tree, targeting a ~1% compiler speedup.
- [#5772](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5772) adds **most missing primitives to fexpr** to support full compiler/stdlib IR dumps.
- [#5761](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5761) proposes **flipping the default for `SPLIT_AROUND_LOOPS`** regalloc parameter.
- [#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762) proposes **enabling prologue shrink-wrap by default**.
- [#5753](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5753) uses **constant propagation to rewrite CFG operations**.
- [#5743](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5743) adds a CFG pass to **move instructions common to several blocks**.
- [#5749](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5749) splits the **CFG preprocessing pass** by moving more definitions.
- [#5747](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5747) fixes a **bug involving effects, callbacks and locals**.
- [#5643](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5643) continues work on **local constraints cannot time travel** as part of the staging+GADTs chain.
- [#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667) (draft) proposes **refining recursive unboxed product dummy layouts** using sort variables.
- [#5752](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5752) works on **ikinds on minus34** branch.
- [#5755](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5755) adds **`val poly_` support** building on the merged `let poly_` work.
- [#5737](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5737) adds **CI configuration with experimental Flambda2 flags** for the testsuite.
- [#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641) was merged: **GADTs mostly sound with respect to staging**, covering parts 1–3 of the staging+GADTs chain.
- [#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629) was merged: **abstract kinds `with kind_` constraints**.
- [#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713) was merged: **`corrupted` and `corruptible` modes**.
- [#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) was merged: **`write` and `writing` modes**.
- [#5724](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5724) was merged: **static modules**.
- [#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639) was merged: **X86 peephole optimization** infrastructure and rules.
- [#5764](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5764), [#5744](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5744), [#5759](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5759) were merged: reaper cleanups including making `-dreaper` more intuitive, removing `{co,}field_{usages,sources}`, and adding profile calls in the solver.
- [#5705](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5705) was merged: **IRC complexity threshold** for register allocation.
- [#5728](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5728) was merged: **new `Lfor` compilation strategy**.
- [#5723](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5723) was merged: **simplify `Cfg_with_layout.insert_block` interface**.
- [#5623](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5623) was merged: **CI test for continuation specialization**.
- [#5710](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5710) was merged: **fixes to the JIT and Opttoploop** for mdx compatibility.
- [#5765](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5765), [#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717), [#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711), [#5729](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5729), [#5694](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5694), [#5534](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5534) were merged as part of the ongoing **OxCaml 5.4 merge track**.
latest_week.discussions_summary: Active design discussions covered patricia tree specialisation performance, the reaper rules architecture, and the n-way join CSE variable strategy.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627) (**Optimized Patricia trees**) saw detailed debate between [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) about the value of specialising `Set` and `Map` vs. fixing the compiler's function specialisation, with concrete benchmarks showing a ~5.7% full-repo speedup. [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened [#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770) as an alternative approach using callback modules.
- [#5733](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5733) / [#5751](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5751) (**n-way join extra params**) involved discussion between [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) on how to best structure the boundary between the join analysis and the join code, leading to the reloaded version with better separation.
- [#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641) (**GADTs with staging**) saw detailed review from [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) covering closure allocations, exception handling in environment traversals, and the fragility of stage tracking in error messages, with a ticket created to track accurate stage information in error traces.
- [#3181](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3181) (**Missed CSE opportunity**) was updated with a new reproducer found during investigation of [#5691](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5691), noting that [#5733](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5733) fixes this with the n-way join.
latest_week.trends_summary: Strong momentum across staging metaprogramming, the modes system expansion, Flambda2/reaper maturation, and systematic backend codegen improvements.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- The **staging and metaprogramming** system is rapidly maturing, with GADTs, local constraints, sound moding, and jkind assertions all landing in a single week, forming a coherent prototype.
- The **modes system** is expanding with new write/corrupted/corruptible modes addressing gaps in implications, while a full **mode polymorphism** system (functional and with levels) is in draft stages in [#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483) and [#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5478).
- The **Flambda2 reaper** is undergoing systematic architectural cleanup (removing redundant state, reorganizing rules, adding profiling) in preparation for enabling it more broadly.
- **Backend codegen improvements** are coming rapidly, with X86 peephole optimizations, improved integer comparison lowering, new CFG passes for duplicate comparisons and block movement, and prologue shrink-wrap all in flight.
- The **5.4 merge track** is advancing steadily with multiple testsuite promotion and conflict-resolution PRs landing each week.

latest_week.brief_summary: Upstream OCaml 5.2.0minus-35 merge bringing metaprogramming type support, plus destruct and mode documentation fixes.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Metaprogramming type handling in locate_types/ptyp_of_type, new mode docs for Write/Writing, and new ignored compiler flags.
latest_week.new_features:
- **Metaprogramming type support** in `locate_types.ml`: `Tquote_eval` is now represented in the type tree using a `Type_ref` node pointing to `Predef.path_eval`, and `Tquote` recursively creates a type tree for its inner type, enabling richer type-at-point output for staged/quoted expressions ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228), [6200664](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/6200664c757a21c96715512051202ea8d639e235))
- **Fixed `ptyp_of_type` for `Tquote_eval`**: the conversion now correctly emits `Typ.constr path_eval [Typ.quote (core_type ty)]` instead of a misrepresented constructor, fixing a review issue raised by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) in [#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) ([#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230))
- **Fixed destruct `Not_found` error** for cross-module GADT patterns: GADT unification inside `destruct.ml` is now wrapped in `Env.with_cmis` to allow CMI loading, working around the `Env.without_cmis` context imposed by `Printtyp.wrap_printing_env` ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228))
- **`Tpat_fun_layout` pattern handling** added to destruct's `subst_patt`, `rm_sub`, `find_branch`, and `Conv.loop`, ensuring layout-polymorphism function patterns are handled safely in destruct operations ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228))
- **New mode documentation entries** in `syntax_doc.ml` contributed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke): `Write` visibility mode ("can be written, but not read") and `Writing` statefulness mode ("can write but not read mutable data") are now documented, and `Observing` statefulness mode is renamed to `Reading` ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228))
- **New ignored compiler flags** added to `mconfig.ml`: `-requires-metaprogramming` and `-uses-metaprogramming` (metaprogramming annotations), plus five x86 peephole optimization flags (`-x86-peephole-optimize`, `-no-x86-peephole-optimize`, `-no-x86-peephole-remove-mov-to-dead-register`, `-no-x86-peephole-remove-redundant-cmp`, `-no-x86-peephole-combine-add-rsp`) to prevent Merlin from rejecting projects that use these upstream compiler options ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228))
latest_week.activity_summary: Upstream sync from oxcaml/oxcaml@5.2.0minus-35 merged, with follow-up formatting and type-conversion fixes; OCaml 5.4 upgrade PR opened.
latest_week.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened [#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227) to upgrade Merlin to OCaml 5.4, which requires a move to ocamlformat 0.29.0 and touches 62 files; formatting changes are isolated in a separate commit for easier review and the PR is currently open and mergeable
- **Upstream sync** `5.2.0minus-35` merged via [#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228): a large import (26,989 additions / 25,285 deletions across 131 files) incorporating upstream OCaml changes including metaprogramming/staging (quote/splice), layout polymorphism (`Tpat_fun_layout`), jkind updates, and mode system extensions; non-trivial conflicts in `typecore.ml` and `ctype.ml` were resolved by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- **Follow-up fix** for `ptyp_of_type` correction noted during review of [#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) was addressed immediately in [#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230) and merged the same day
- **Formatting fix** [#229](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/229) was self-merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) without review, noted as a trivial change
latest_week.discussions_summary: Review of #228 identified a Tquote_eval representation bug; jbachurski flagged metaprogramming review needs for staging and layout polymorphism changes.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) flagged in [#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) that the `Tquote_eval` handling in `ptyp_of_type` was incorrect, explaining that `Tquote_eval t` represents `Tconstr(Predef.path_eval, [Tquote t], _)` and that the fix should use `Typ.quote (core_type ty)`; this was promptly addressed in [#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230)
- The [#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228) PR description calls out that the `Tquote_eval`-related and `Tpat_fun_layout`-related upstream changes warrant expert review from those working on metaprogramming and layout polymorphism respectively, indicating ongoing coordination needs with the broader oxcaml team
latest_week.trends_summary: Continued cadence of upstream OCaml syncs with Merlin-specific conflict resolution, alongside incremental metaprogramming type support.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Upstream sync cadence**: the merge of `5.2.0minus-35` follows a recurring pattern of importing large upstream batches and resolving conflicts in Merlin-specific analysis code (destruct, locate_types, ptyp_of_type), suggesting this is a regular maintenance burden ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228))
- **Metaprogramming surface area growing**: quote/splice (`Tquote`, `Tquote_eval`, `Tsplice`) type constructors are appearing across multiple analysis modules; each upstream sync requires Merlin to explicitly handle these new type forms to avoid regressions in type-at-point and destruct ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228), [#230](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/230))
- **Compiler flag allowlist maintenance**: the addition of metaprogramming and x86 peephole flags to the ignored-flags list in `mconfig.ml` reflects a pattern where new upstream compiler features require Merlin to be updated to avoid rejecting valid build configurations ([#228](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/228))
latest_week.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson drove all activity this week, with review input from Jakub Bachurski and documentation contributions from Nailen Matschke.

latest_week.brief_summary: New PR adds configurable eventfd/pipe creation in Linux and Posix backends; two open issues received discussion.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Optional eventfd/pipe parameters added to Linux and Posix backend run functions for shell and tool authors.
latest_week.new_features:
- **Configurable eventfd creation** in the Linux backend: `run_event_loop` and `run` in `lib_eio_linux` now accept an optional `?eventfd` parameter, allowing callers to supply a custom function for creating event file descriptors ([#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836), [807ad3d](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/commit/807ad3dd0c60e1892dae71706411eac219bbfb7c))
- **Configurable pipe creation** in the Posix backend: `lib_eio_posix` domain manager and scheduler now accept an optional `?pipe` parameter, enabling callers to control how pipes are created — useful for shells that need to move FDs to higher numbers (e.g. `dash savefd` pattern) ([#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836), [807ad3d](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/commit/807ad3dd0c60e1892dae71706411eac219bbfb7c))
latest_week.activity_summary: PR #836 opened to make backend event FDs configurable; two longstanding issues received follow-up comments.
latest_week.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) **Make Linux and Posix eventfd functions configurable**, a currently unmerged PR affecting 8 files (35 additions, 26 deletions) to support shells like `msh` that need to relocate FDs to higher numbers
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) commented on the long-running [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) **Make Waiters lock-free**, noting that part of the referenced algorithm is already in use via `cells.mli`
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) engaged with [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) **Eio.Path.load race condition and /proc failure**, suggesting it overlaps with [#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798) and proposing to clarify issue scope
latest_week.discussions_summary: Discussion on Eio.Path.load race condition and /proc issue clarified overlap with existing issue #798.
latest_week.notable_discussions:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) reported that `Eio.Path.load` uses file size as an exact buffer bound rather than a hint, causing **silent truncation on race conditions** and returning empty strings for `/proc` virtual files ([#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835)); [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) acknowledged the overlap with [#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798) and suggested splitting the race condition concern into a dedicated issue
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted on [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) that the `cells.mli` module already implements part of the lock-free waiter design described in the referenced Kotlin coroutines paper
latest_week.trends_summary: Shell integration use cases are surfacing requirements for finer control over low-level FD management in backends.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Backend extensibility for system-level tools**: [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) reflects a pattern where shell authors and low-level tool writers need hooks into how Eio creates internal file descriptors, pointing toward a broader need for configurable backend primitives
- **`Eio.Path.load` correctness gap**: both [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) and [#798](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/798) highlight that `Path.load`'s stat-then-read approach is unsuitable for virtual filesystems and concurrent environments, suggesting a fix is becoming a priority
latest_week.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris opened PR #836 and commented on #835; Thomas Leonard and Volker Diels-Grabsch discussed open issues.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored the feature commit and opened [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) for configurable backend event FDs, and provided triage feedback on [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835)
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) contributed discussion on [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382), clarifying the current state of the lock-free waiters design
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) filed detailed bug report [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) documenting the `Eio.Path.load` race condition and `/proc` incompatibility with a reproducible example
